assertions.description: CSS parser accepts a comma-separated list of container conditions inside @container via CSS.supports.
An @container rule with a comma-separated list of queries parses into a CSSContainerRule and round-trips through cssText.
assertions.test: (()=>{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@container (min-width: 100px), (min-height: 100px) { div { color: red } }');const r=s.cssRules[0];return r && r.constructor.name==='CSSContainerRule';})()

assertions.description: CSSContainerRule is exposed on the window so script can introspect @container rules.
The conditionText of a comma-separated @container rule preserves the disjunction.
assertions.test: (()=>{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@container (min-width: 100px), (min-height: 100px) { div { color: red } }');const r=s.cssRules[0];return typeof r.conditionText==='string' && r.conditionText.includes(',');})()

assertions.description: A comma list where one branch references an unknown feature still parses because at-least-one-matches semantics tolerate unknown branches.
assertions.test: (()=>{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@container (totally-unknown-feature: 1), (min-width: 1px) { div { color: red } }');return s.cssRules.length===1;})()
